What it does: Same questions, the urgent-for-you one FIRST
Why it's the moat: The most powerful and most underused. An expert never hides questions in a call; they LEAD with what is on fire for you. Show/hide alone can't copy this.
A solo ager never sees guardian questions; a parent does. The list feels made for you, not a generic form.

Feels like (to the member): "It went deep on my actual answer"
Why it's the moat: One answer opens a different, deeper path than another. The interview adapts as you go, like a real conversation.

What it does: Pure show/hide is NOT the moat
Why it's the moat: If ~70% of 40-per-domain questions are universal (everyone makes a will), hiding the other 30% still shows everyone almost the same list. The moat is the THREE levers together, only one of which is show/hide.

What it does: Order = (1) why-now signal target -> (2) who-you're-responsible-for cluster -> (3) base order
Why it's the moat: The expert never opens with the bank. They find the why-now and the who-you're-responsible-for, and lead with THAT. Evidence-backed across all case studies.

What it does: Same signals + same answers -> same order, every time
Why it's the moat: Auditable and defensible. Not a black-box model guessing; a transparent rules engine an expert can sign off on. Regulated-friendly.
